name: competing-hypotheses-debug
summary: 3 parallel investigators pursue different root-cause hypotheses; an Opus synthesizer picks the winner.
when_to_use: Tricky bugs where the cause is genuinely unclear and multiple theories are plausible. Not for straightforward bugs.

file_ownership.investigators: each writes to .claude/investigations/{role}.md — evidence table, ruled-in/ruled-out, proposed fix
file_ownership.synthesizer: reads all three; writes .claude/investigations/VERDICT.md with winning hypothesis + confidence + fix sketch

coordination: 1. User states the bug + 2-3 plausible hypotheses.
2. Launch 3 investigators in parallel (one Agent tool call per hypothesis).
3. After all 3 finish, synthesizer reads the 3 investigation files and picks a winner.
4. User decides whether to proceed to implementation or dig deeper.
worktree_guidance: 3 parallel worktrees — investigators must not observe each other's findings during investigation.
cost_estimate: ~$2-4. Parallel investigator passes are cheap; synthesizer pass is the expensive one.
anti_patterns: Do not let investigators talk to each other — the independence is the point.
Do not pre-seed a hypothesis with 'probably X' — bias defeats the approach.
